mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market health that increases disposable income expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns do not exceed 50% of observations during declining UNRATE periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 240, elevated inflation forces upward revisions to discount rates and compresses real earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 240, or Rank IC is greater than -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising unemployment and declining industrial production signal a joint deterioration in labor and supply conditions that amplifies recession risk beyond either variable alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising UNRATE and falling INDPRO does not produce significantly more negative 60-day returns than either signal alone (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC falls below 0.03 in magnitude over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is below 5.0%, increases in retail sales signal demand overheating that triggers Federal Reserve tightening expectations and discount-rate repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE below 5.0%, rising RSAFS does not precede negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or Rank IC exceeds -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 105 index level signals acute manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate revenue visibility and capacity utilization, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO is below 105, or Rank IC is greater than -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payroll employment signal durable income growth and consumption expansion that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive following PAYEMS increases (t-test p > 0.05), or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1250 thousand units signals weakening residential investment momentum, a leading component of aggregate demand and construction employment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC is greater than -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 0.5 percent signals that monetary policy is no longer accommodative, raising the equity discount rate and corporate borrowing costs, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5%, or Rank IC is greater than -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
